Oracle Events
OE 1: Use quickbooks_search_bills and quickbooks_get_bill for ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC, HM-2026-Q1-0042, and HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD. Confirm PHMSA and Mosaic accrual totals, the $12,800 Heartland bill, and three DISPUTED lines including the Swift not Heartland row before trusting chat.
OE 2: Use quickbooks_search_bills and quickbooks_get_bill for STIP-2026-0089, STIP-2026-0094 plus its admin-hold bill, and STIP-2026-0097 plus its hold variant. Reconcile Kevin Tran paid $89 ErgoKey, Andre Washington held $89 ErgoKey, Jordan Ekwueme held $45 StandPro, against Airtable stipends next.
OE 3: Use quickbooks_search_accounts to locate Regulatory Compliance DOT Penalties (Pending) tied to ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001. Expect about $50,000 liability balance and wording that matches the PHMSA penalty accrual bill narrative in the books.
OE 4: Run search_emails in INBOX and again in SENT on PHMSA-2026-04-1187, Linda Castellano, June 5 2026 extension, and Marcus or Chloe DOT documentation work. Open full bodies with get_email_by_id or get_thread when summaries are insufficient.
OE 5: search_emails in INBOX and SENT for Dmitri Volkov stipend audit to Marcus Thorne and Marcus replies to Dmitri, Mina Hashimoto, and Emeka Diallo on TechComfort duplicate receipts, stipend holds, and client HR verification outreach.
OE 6: search_emails in INBOX and SENT for Heartland HM-2026-Q1-0042 dispute, corrected bill expectations, or payment hold language from Marcus or ops, then align phrasing with QuickBooks HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD and Slack.
OE 7: conversations_search_messages for HM-2026-Q1-0042 Heartland Q1 bill, Chloe Vance relocation reconciliation, and Marcus Thorne hold or DISPUTED line notes. Use conversations_replies on threads so $4,800 dispute logic matches QuickBooks line text.
OE 8: airtable_search_records or airtable_get_record for rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, and rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand. Compare duplicate-hash and template flags plus approval fields to QuickBooks stipend bills from OE 2.
OE 9: crm_search_companies and crm_get_company for Mosaic Robotics; crm_list_engagements with company_ids company_mosaic. Read AM narrative on relocations, surcharges, and damage before composing the finance NOTE in OE 13.
OE 10: crm_search_companies and crm_get_company for NorthWind Technologies; crm_list_engagements with company_ids company_northwind. Capture Raj Patel Class 9 lithium UPS and PHMSA documentation context AMs logged before composing the finance NOTE in OE 14.
OE 11: linear_get_project with query project_dot_compliance_audit or linear_list_projects; linear_list_issues with project set to project_dot_compliance_audit for the DOT Compliance Audit Q1 Q2 2026 Hazmat Shipments scope; linear_get_issue issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic for placarding gap and Tanaka laser Swift details.
OE 12: contacts_search_contacts for Hana Kim, Alejandro Fuentes, Elena Rostova, and Marcus Thorne; confirm hana.kim@moveops.com, alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, elena.rostova@moveops.com, and marcus.thorne@moveops.com for calendar and mail steps.
OE 13: crm_create_engagement with engagement_type NOTE, company_ids company_mosaic, body summarizing $90,000 ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC damage accrual, Andre Washington STIP-2026-0094 hold, and PHMSA-2026-04-1187 linkage to Keiko Tanaka laser; preserve AM storyline.
OE 14: crm_create_engagement with engagement_type NOTE, company_ids company_northwind, body covering Raj Patel Class 9 lithium UPS documentation status and PHMSA inquiry context only without rewriting prior AM engagement narrative.
OE 15: linear_list_comments with issueId issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic to scan existing thread text, then linear_create_comment with issueId issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic and body covering ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 booking for PHMSA-2026-04-1187, missing placarding certificate on Tanaka Mosaic laser shipment, June 5 2026 PHMSA deadline versus May 21 original cutoff, without duplicating prior ops-only notes.
OE 16: calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to or calendar_search_events across 2026-04-27 afternoon on Marcus Thorne calendar; choose a conflict-free forty-five minute window then calendar_add_calendar_event titled Q2 Contingent Liability Review with attendees Hana Kim and Alejandro Fuentes using emails from OE 12; set start_datetime and end_datetime to the chosen gap.
OE 17: send_email from marcus.thorne@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com summarizing four threads with QuickBooks figures, $90k Mosaic and $50k DOT accruals as top priorities over Heartland dispute and stipend holds, chatter mismatches, and hold or escalate advice for another week.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome

✗Run #1The Linear comment includes ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, the $50,000 accrual, June 5, 2026 as the extended deadline, and the placarding certificate gap. However, it does not mention May 21 as the original cutoff deadline — the criterion requires both dates be present. Only the June 5 extended deadline appears in the comment.
✗Run #2The Linear comment body includes ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, the $50,000 penalty accrual, the placarding certificate gap, and June 5, 2026 as the revised deadline. However, it does not mention May 21 as the original cutoff — it only says 'PHMSA granted a 15-calendar-day extension' without naming the original deadline date. The criterion requires both the extended deadline and May 21 as the original cutoff.
✗Run #3The Linear comment includes ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, the $50,000 penalty accrual, the placarding certificate gap, and June 5, 2026 as the extended deadline. However, the comment does not mention May 21 as the original (pre-extension) cutoff — it only notes '15-day extension granted by Linda Castellano, PHMSA' without specifying the prior date. The criterion requires both dates.
✗Run #4The Linear comment includes ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, the $50,000 penalty, and the June 5, 2026 deadline, but does NOT mention the original May 21 cutoff date. Only the June 5 extension deadline is referenced.

The criterion requires both dates.
✗Run #6The Linear comment body includes ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, $50,000, the placarding certificate gap, and June 5, 2026 as the extended deadline. However, May 21 as the original cutoff is NOT mentioned anywhere in the comment. The comment only says 'PHMSA granted a 15-calendar-day extension' without specifying the original deadline date.
